executive_summary.recommended_approach: Deploy Autonomize AI's multi-agent PA Copilot integrated with the payer's AWS infrastructure via an event-driven integration layer (Kafka + FHIR R4 facade), enabling 60% auto-determination of prior authorization requests while maintaining full HIPAA compliance and CMS-0057-F readiness.
key_benefits: 60% auto-determination rate reduces PA turnaround from 5 days to < 24 hours
$11.7M annual savings from 40% FTE reduction (180 of 450 FTEs)
CMS-0057-F compliance via FHIR R4 PA API before January 2027 deadline
Multi-tenant architecture supports 20 LOBs without re-architecture
Proven platform — Autonomize AI already serves 3 of top 5 US health plans
key_risks: Fax OCR accuracy on handwritten notes may require manual fallback for 10-15% of faxed requests
Legacy clinical database integration complexity — 60% of clinical data in non-FHIR systems
12-week timeline is aggressive for full integration build — phased LOB rollout mitigates
Auto-denial excluded from Phase 1 — regulatory review required before Phase 2 activation

llm.primary_model: Autonomize AI PA Copilot multi-agent pipeline (proprietary)
llm.fallback_model: Amazon Bedrock Claude for supplementary clinical NLP
llm.hosting: Autonomize AI managed platform with secure connectivity to payer VPC
llm.rationale: Autonomize AI provides purpose-built healthcare AI agents optimized for PA workflows. Amazon Bedrock supplements with general-purpose clinical NLP for document processing. Both are HIPAA-eligible.

orchestration.framework: Autonomize AI Genesis Platform + AWS Step Functions
orchestration.rationale: Autonomize AI Genesis orchestrates the PA-specific multi-agent pipeline (ingestion, NLP, eligibility, guidelines, decision). AWS Step Functions orchestrates the integration workflow between payer systems and the Autonomize AI platform.

frontend.rationale: PA determination integrates into existing provider portal and clinical reviewer workflows. No new user-facing application required in Phase 1.

operational_db.purpose: PA request records, determination history, reviewer assignments

config_and_audit.purpose: LOB configuration rules, immutable audit trail, real-time lookups

document_store.purpose: Fax images, clinical documents, ML model artifacts

analytics.purpose: Historical PA analytics, reporting, MLOps training data

clinical_data.purpose: FHIR R4 data store for standardized clinical records, CMS-0057-F compliance

infrastructure.logging: CloudWatch Logs with structured JSON, 30-day hot retention

component_design.name: API Gateway
component_design.purpose: Secure entry point for provider portal PA submissions and FHIR R4 PA API (CMS-0057-F). Rate limiting, authentication, request validation.
inputs: FHIR R4 PA requests (Da Vinci PAS)
outputs: Validated PA request to Ingestion Service
component_design.scalability: Managed auto-scaling (API Gateway handles burst traffic natively)

component_design.name: Document Processing Service
component_design.technology: AWS Lambda + Amazon Textract + Amazon Comprehend Medical
component_design.purpose: OCR extraction from faxed documents (Textract), clinical entity extraction from unstructured text (Comprehend Medical), X12 278 parsing.
outputs: Structured PA request data with extracted clinical entities
component_design.scalability: Lambda auto-scales per document; Textract async API for batch fax processing

component_design.name: Data Normalizer
component_design.purpose: Normalize multi-format PA requests into unified PA Request schema. Validate completeness, map codes (ICD-10, CPT, HCPCS).
outputs: Unified PA Request object published to Kafka

component_design.name: Event Bus
component_design.purpose: Decoupled event streaming backbone. Topics: pa-requests, pa-enriched, pa-determinations, pa-responses, audit-events. Enables replay, ordering, and parallel consumer groups.
outputs: Events consumed by PA Copilot, Response Router, Audit Service, Analytics
component_design.scalability: Kafka partitioning by LOB ID enables parallel processing across 20 LOBs

component_design.name: FHIR R4 Facade
component_design.technology: AWS HealthLake + FastAPI FHIR client + Legacy Data Adapters
component_design.purpose: Unified clinical data access layer. Queries FHIR R4 endpoints via SMART on FHIR auth, transforms legacy DB records into FHIR R4 resources. Aggregates into Clinical Evidence Package per patient.
inputs: Patient ID + clinical data request from PA Copilot
outputs: FHIR R4 Bundle (Patient, Condition, Observation, DiagnosticReport, MedicationRequest)
component_design.scalability: Horizontal via ECS Fargate tasks; connection pooling for legacy DB access

component_design.name: Eligibility Service
component_design.purpose: Real-time member eligibility verification against TriZetto Facets. Checks enrollment, benefit plan, service-specific coverage, and contract rules.
inputs: Member ID, service code, date of service
component_design.scalability: Horizontal scaling with DynamoDB DAX cache for frequent lookups (5-min TTL)

component_design.name: PA Copilot Orchestrator
component_design.purpose: Multi-agent PA processing pipeline. Coordinates specialized agents: Document Agent, Clinical NLP Agent, Eligibility Agent, Guidelines Matching Agent, Decision Agent. Produces PA determination with confidence score and clinical rationale.
inputs: Clinical Evidence Package from FHIR Facade
outputs: PA Determination (approve/pend) with confidence score, rationale, guideline citations
component_design.scalability: Managed by Autonomize AI platform; scales with agent instance count

component_design.name: Guidelines Matching Service
component_design.purpose: Query InterQual and MCG clinical guidelines APIs. Match PA request clinical evidence against applicable criteria. Return match result with specific guideline citations.
inputs: Clinical evidence summary, diagnosis codes, procedure codes
outputs: Guideline match result with citations and coverage determination
component_design.scalability: Horizontal scaling; caching of guideline criteria sets (updated quarterly)

component_design.name: Human Review Workflow
component_design.technology: AWS Step Functions + existing clinical review tools
component_design.purpose: Route low-confidence cases (< 0.85) to clinical reviewers. Present pre-assembled evidence package. Capture reviewer decision and corrections for feedback loop.
outputs: Correction data for MLOps feedback loop
component_design.scalability: Step Functions manages concurrent review workflows; scales with reviewer capacity

component_design.name: Response Router
component_design.purpose: Deliver PA determination back to originating channel: portal notification, X12 278 response, or fax response letter. Format-aware routing based on inbound channel.
component_design.scalability: Lambda auto-scales; SNS fan-out for multi-channel delivery

component_design.name: Audit Service
component_design.purpose: Immutable audit trail for every PA decision. Captures: decision maker (AI/human), data considered, guidelines applied, confidence score, overrides, timestamps. Cryptographic hash chain for tamper evidence.
inputs: Audit events from all components via Kafka (audit-events topic)
outputs: Audit data replicated to Snowflake for analytics
component_design.scalability: DynamoDB on-demand scales automatically; Lambda processes audit events asynchronously

component_design.name: LOB Configuration Engine
component_design.purpose: Configuration-driven rules engine for 20 LOBs. Stores LOB-specific: approval thresholds, guideline sets, workflow steps, confidence thresholds, auto-determination eligibility. Version-controlled with rollback.
outputs: LOB-specific configuration consumed by PA Copilot and Decision Agent
component_design.scalability: DynamoDB handles config reads at any scale; DAX cache for hot path

component_design.name: MLOps Pipeline
component_design.technology: Amazon SageMaker (Model Monitor, Processing, Training, Endpoints)
component_design.purpose: Production ML lifecycle: drift detection (PSI, KS test daily), outcome monitoring (weekly), reviewer feedback aggregation (daily), model fine-tuning (monthly), A/B testing before promotion.
component_design.scalability: SageMaker managed scaling; spot instances for training jobs

component_design.name: Analytics and Dashboards
component_design.purpose: Operational dashboards: PA volume, turnaround time, auto-determination rate, clinical accuracy, per-LOB breakdowns. Executive, operations, and clinical dashboard views.
inputs: PA processing events replicated from Kafka to Snowflake via CDC
component_design.scalability: Snowflake auto-scaling warehouses; QuickSight SPICE in-memory caching

data_flows.name: Fax Ingestion
data_flows.destination: S3 → Textract → Comprehend Medical → Normalizer → Kafka
data_flows.data_type: TIFF/PDF → Extracted text → Clinical entities → Unified PA Request

data_flows.name: Portal PA Submission
data_flows.data_type: JSON PA Request → Unified PA Request

data_flows.name: EDI X12 278 Ingestion
data_flows.data_type: ASC X12 278 → Parsed segments → Unified PA Request

data_flows.name: Clinical Data Retrieval (FHIR)
data_flows.protocol: FHIR R4 REST API (SMART on FHIR OAuth 2.0)
data_flows.data_type: FHIR R4 Resources (Patient, Condition, Observation, DiagnosticReport)
data_flows.encryption: TLS 1.2 + SMART on FHIR token scoping

data_flows.name: Clinical Data Retrieval (Legacy)
data_flows.source: Legacy Clinical Databases (Oracle, SQL Server, DB2)
data_flows.destination: Legacy Data Adapter → FHIR R4 transformation → Clinical Evidence Package

data_flows.name: PA Determination Flow
data_flows.data_type: PA Determination with rationale, confidence, guideline citations

data_flows.name: Audit Event Stream
data_flows.encryption: Kafka TLS + DynamoDB encryption at rest

data_flows.name: MLOps Feedback Loop
data_flows.data_type: Reviewer corrections, outcome data, prediction logs

data_flows.name: Analytics Pipeline
data_flows.data_type: PA processing events, audit records, performance metrics

cloud_infrastructure.deployment_pattern: Container-based services on ECS Fargate for steady-state workloads + Lambda for event-driven processing (S3 triggers, Kafka consumers, audit event processing). Step Functions for workflow orchestration.

dr_strategy.approach: Multi-AZ within us-east-1 for HA. Cross-region S3 replication to us-west-2 for documents. RDS automated backups with cross-region snapshot copy. DynamoDB global tables for audit trail. MSK mirroring for Kafka topic replication.
dr_strategy.failover: Automated RDS failover within region. Manual cross-region failover for full DR scenario.

cost_optimization.compute: Fargate (no idle EC2) + Lambda (per-invocation pricing). Spot instances for SageMaker training.
cost_optimization.storage: S3 Intelligent-Tiering for documents. DynamoDB on-demand mode. RDS reserved instances for steady-state.
cost_optimization.monitoring: CloudWatch 30-day hot retention, archive to S3 Glacier for compliance retention.
cost_optimization.tags: Cost allocation tags per LOB for chargeback. Monthly cost reviews.

tool_gateway.description: Amazon API Gateway + AWS Lambda serves as the tool gateway, converting external API calls (Facets, InterQual, MCG, FHIR endpoints) into tool invocations for Autonomize AI agents. Centralized auth, rate limiting, and circuit breakers.

identity_layer.description: Multi-layer authentication: SMART on FHIR (OAuth 2.0) for clinical data access, mTLS for Autonomize AI platform connectivity, SAML 2.0 for clinical reviewer SSO, service accounts for system-to-system. JWT tokens with 15-minute expiry for JIT access.

execution_runtime.description: Autonomize AI Genesis Platform provides the multi-agent execution runtime. Integration services run on ECS Fargate. Event processing on Lambda. Workflow orchestration via Step Functions.

persistent_memory.description: DynamoDB for real-time state (PA request status, configuration). RDS PostgreSQL for relational PA records. S3 for document storage and ML artifacts. Snowflake for historical analytics and training data. AWS HealthLake for FHIR R4 clinical data store.

multi_agent_pipeline.description: Autonomize AI PA Copilot orchestrates specialized agents in sequence: (1) Document Agent — OCR and entity extraction, (2) Clinical NLP Agent — clinical note understanding, (3) Eligibility Agent — member verification, (4) Guidelines Agent — criteria matching, (5) Decision Agent — determination with confidence scoring, (6) Response Agent — multi-format delivery. Supervisor-worker pattern with the PA Copilot as supervisor.

logging.format: Structured JSON with correlation ID (pa_request_id) across all services
logging.aggregation: CloudWatch Logs with Log Insights for querying
logging.retention: 30 days hot (CloudWatch), 7 years cold (S3 Glacier) for HIPAA compliance
logging.phi_handling: PHI fields masked in logs. Full PHI available only in audit trail with elevated access.

application: PA request volume (per LOB, per channel)

tracing.scope: End-to-end request tracing from ingestion through determination delivery
tracing.correlation: pa_request_id propagated across all services and Kafka messages
tracing.sampling: 100% for errors, 10% for successful requests in production

notes.operational_excellence: Strong: event-driven architecture, structured logging, blue/green deployment, CloudWatch dashboards. Gap: runbooks and incident response procedures not yet defined. Recommendation: document runbooks during integration build phase.
notes.security: Strong: multi-layer auth (SMART on FHIR, mTLS, SAML), PHI field-level encryption, immutable audit trail, WAF on API Gateway, PrivateLink for network isolation. Gap: penetration testing and security certification timeline not specified. Recommendation: schedule pen test before go-live.
notes.reliability: Strong: multi-AZ ECS, Kafka event durability with replay, circuit breakers, human fallback path. Gap: cross-region DR is manual failover — not automated. Recommendation: automate DR failover with Route 53 health checks for Phase 2.
notes.performance_efficiency: Strong: event-driven async processing, DynamoDB DAX caching, Kafka partitioning by LOB. Gap: 30-second P95 target is generous — could optimize with pre-computed clinical evidence caching. Recommendation: benchmark actual latency in QA/UAT.
notes.cost_optimization: Strong: serverless-first (Fargate, Lambda), S3 lifecycle policies, DynamoDB on-demand, Snowflake auto-suspend. Gap: Autonomize AI platform licensing cost not quantified. Recommendation: negotiate usage-based pricing aligned with auto-determination volume.
notes.sustainability: Strong: serverless eliminates idle compute, multi-tenant shares infra across 20 LOBs, batch MLOps reduces training compute. Gap: no carbon-aware scheduling or renewable energy commitments documented.

well_architected_scores.score_source: self-assessment (preliminary) — parallel-wa-reviewer agents running, scores will be calibrated
well_architected_scores.calibration_note: Preliminary self-assessment scores. Will be replaced by parallel-wa-reviewer sub-agent scores when they complete. Self-assessment may overestimate by 1-2 points.

alternatives_considered.name: Monolithic PA Processing Application
alternatives_considered.summary: Single application handling all PA processing logic without multi-agent decomposition
alternatives_considered.why_rejected: Cannot scale independently per function. Harder to customize per LOB. Does not leverage Autonomize AI's agent marketplace. Contradicts the event-driven, microservices architecture needed for 2.5M requests/month.

alternatives_considered.name: Multi-Instance Per LOB
alternatives_considered.summary: Deploy separate PA processing stack per line of business (20 instances)
alternatives_considered.why_rejected: 20x infrastructure cost. Operational overhead of managing 20 deployments. Inconsistent updates across LOBs. Paul explicitly chose multi-tenant with config isolation — shared platform is more cost-effective for same-payer LOBs.

alternatives_considered.name: Direct Legacy DB Access (No FHIR Facade)
alternatives_considered.summary: Connect AI system directly to legacy clinical databases without FHIR standardization layer
alternatives_considered.why_rejected: Creates tight coupling to legacy schemas. Does not prepare for CMS-0057-F FHIR API requirement. Every legacy system change would require AI pipeline changes. FHIR facade provides abstraction and future-proofing.

alternatives_considered.name: Batch Processing Instead of Event-Driven
alternatives_considered.summary: Process PA requests in hourly or daily batches instead of real-time event streaming
alternatives_considered.why_rejected: Cannot achieve < 24-hour turnaround target. CMS-0057-F requires real-time PA status inquiry. Provider experience degrades with batch delays. Kafka event streaming provides both real-time and replay capabilities.

alternatives_considered.name: Full Retraining Instead of Fine-Tuning for MLOps
alternatives_considered.summary: Retrain the PA decision model from scratch on every feedback cycle
alternatives_considered.why_rejected: Excessive compute cost for monthly cycles. Risk of catastrophic forgetting. Fine-tuning on reviewer corrections preserves base model knowledge while adapting to drift. Full retraining reserved for major guideline version changes (annual).

_metadata.author: sa-agent
_metadata.design_method: event-driven microservices with multi-agent AI orchestration
